dataset_info.name: Traditions
dataset_info.description: Indigenous and traditional practices for collective healing, decision-making, and community development.

records.name: Ubuntu Indaba Circles
records.description: Elder-led communal dialogues for relational maturity.
records.preparation: The community gathers under a central tree; elders consult ancestors. Grievances are temporally set aside to focus on systemic issues.
records.agenda: 1. Invocation of lineages. 2. Statement of the disruption. 3. Round-robin speaking without interruption. 4. Elder synthesis focusing on 'we' restoration. 5. Communal meal.
key_questions: How does this fracture affect the collective whole?
What part of my ego is causing harm to our shared fabric?
records.awareness_development: Shifts attention aggressively from 'me/mine' to 'us/ours'. Demands the cognitive capacity to hold multiple opposing communal viewpoints simultaneously without immediate judgment.

records.name: Medicine Wheel Ceremonies
records.description: First Nations circles for holistic stages.
records.preparation: Constructing the physical stone wheel aligned with the cardinal directions. Smudging with sage to clear the mind.
records.agenda: 1. Honoring the East (Mind/Spring). 2. Honoring the South (Body/Summer). 3. Honoring the West (Emotion/Autumn). 4. Honoring the North (Spirit/Winter).
key_questions: In which quadrant of my life is my energy stagnating?
How do the cycles of nature reflect my internal maturation?
records.awareness_development: Teaches cyclical and systemic thinking. Forces the individual to view their development not as a linear upward climb, but as a balanced, multidimensional wheel requiring holistic integration.

records.name: Dreamtime Yarning Circles
records.preparation: Sitting on Country (specific sacred land). Acknowledgment of traditional custodians and ancestral spirits.
records.agenda: 1. Deep listening (Dadirri). 2. Non-linear storytelling interweaving past, present, and myth. 3. Silence and absorption. 4. Collective sense-making.
key_questions: Where does my personal story intersect with the songlines of the land?
Who am I in deep time?
records.awareness_development: Stretches temporal awareness massively. Individuals learn to perceive their life not as a contained timeline, but as an active, living continuation of ancestral 'Dreaming'.

records.name: Temazcal Sweat Lodges
records.preparation: Heating volcanic stones; setting intention for purification.
records.agenda: 1. Crawling into the dark dome (womb). 2. Pouring water on stones (four doors/stages). 3. Chanting and crying out. 4. Emerging reborn.
key_questions: What parts of my identity must die in the heat?
records.awareness_development: Uses extreme physical sensation to bypass mental defenses, creating deep communal bonding through shared vulnerability.

records.name: Bhavana Group Meditation
records.description: Buddhist sangha retreats for insight progression.
records.agenda: 1. Alternating sitting/walking meditation. 2. Dharma talks. 3. Teacher interviews. 4. Group dedication of merit.
key_questions: Is my suffering distinct from the group's suffering?
records.awareness_development: Cultivates profound meta-awareness and the realization of non-self (Anatta).

records.name: Palaver Huts
records.preparation: Gathering in the open-sided community structure.
records.agenda: 1. Presenting a community dilemma. 2. Youth offer modern perspectives. 3. Elders offer historical precedent. 4. Synthesis.
key_questions: How do we honor the past while adapting to the future?
records.awareness_development: Develops dialectical thinking—the ability to hold and merge contradictory generational truths.
